commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

2026-05-24 native Windows proof from this branch, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`:

- Native Windows Node: `v22.22.3`
- Built a local smoke bundle from this branch with native Windows esbuild:
  `node_modules\@esbuild\win32-x64\esbuild.exe src\entry.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --packages=external --outfile=.\oc85739-entry.mjs`
- Ran the affected startup/status path with shell-env fallback enabled:
  `$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'; $env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'; node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status`
  - exit code: `0`
  - stderr: empty
  - stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT`: `False`
  - stdout includes `OpenClaw status` and `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`
- Direct fallback proof from the same native Windows checkout also returned:
  `{ "platform": "win32", "result": { "ok": true, "applied": [] }, "warnings": [] }`

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`acf265d4d5`), head `8ef82383f5`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 21 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Native Windows environment tested: Windows 10.0.26200 x64, Node `22.22.3`, checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-85739-win` on branch `fix-84795-win32-shell-env-fallback`, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`.

Exact affected command run after this patch:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'
$env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'
node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status
```

Evidence after fix: the command exited `0`, stderr was empty, stdout started with `OpenClaw status`, and a captured stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT` returned `False`. The status output reported `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`, proving the smoke ran on native Windows rather than WSL.

Observed result after fix: native Windows status starts normally, the shell-env fallback does not attempt `/bin/sh`, and no ENOENT fallback warning is emitted. Direct module proof of `loadShellEnvFallback` on the same machine also returned `{ ok: true, applied: [] }` with no warnings.

What was not tested: I did not run the globally installed `openclaw` package. The smoke used a local bundle from this PR checkout because this Windows machine intermittently denies pnpm/esbuild postinstall child-process shims, while the native Windows entry bundle and Node runtime execute correctly.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.author: latensified

target.title: telegram: allow last blockquote to be expandable
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #84082 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Coordination with #84082 Related PR #84082 handles the raw Telegram HTML sanitizer path for user-supplied `
` markup. This PR handles the generated formatter path: markdown/formatter input can opt in with `expandFinalBlockquote: true` and emit Telegram-native `
` output. These paths are complementary and can land separately. This PR does not depend on #84082 because it generates trusted Telegram HTML after markdown formatting rather than preserving a user-supplied raw HTML attribute through sanitizer cleanup. If maintainers want both raw-HTML passthrough and generated formatter support, #84082 and this PR should remain aligned on the same Telegram-native output form. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Live formatter command output plus Telegram screenshots: - Collapsed screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b590a21f-5b43-4bea-b917-9bba48fa42e2 - Expanded screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77508c27-954a-40bb-a0ae-e4666a08d95e - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with collapsed and expanded screenshots. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the live formatter command and Telegram screenshots. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Collapsed: Expanded: ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.
target.author: latensified

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
